# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve, ServingContext } from "https://deno.land/std@0.172.0/http/server.ts";
import { Permissions } from "https://deno.land/std@0.172.0/permissions.ts";

async function fetchData(url: string): Promise<Response> {
  const networkPermissions = await Permissions.request({ name: "net" });
  if (!networkPermissions.granted) throw new Error("Network permissions denied.");

  const response = await Deno.fetch(url);
  if (response.status >= 400 && response.status < 600) {
    throw new Error(`Error fetching data from ${url}: ${response.statusText}`);
  }

  return response;
}

const app = serve((ctx: ServingContext) => {
  const url = new URLSearchParams(ctx.request.url).get("url") || "";
  if (!url) ctx.respond({ status: 400, body: "URL parameter is missing." });
  else fetchData(url)
    .then((response) => ctx.respond(response))
    .catch((error) => ctx.respond({ status: 500, body: error.message }));
});

console.log("Listening on port 8000...");
